message:
test(iconv): host-parity differential harness + spec_reference fixture

crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/iconv_phase1_conformance_test.rs
  * Adds a differential execution harness to the iconv phase-1
    conformance test: each fixture case is now decoded into a
    `MatrixCaseEnvelope { kind, run: Option<DifferentialExecution>,
    error: Option<String> }`, and when `kind == "differential"` the
    harness compares the frankenlibc output (`impl_output`) against
    the host glibc output flag (`host_parity`). Cases missing a
    `run` block but present with `error` exercise the failure
    classification path instead.
  * Imports `std::process::{Command, Stdio}` for the subprocess
    branch that shells out to `iconv(1)` as the host oracle when
    the case requests byte-for-byte parity.
  * `FixtureFile` struct gains a `#[serde(default)] spec_reference`
    field to surface the fixture's POSIX reference in failure
    diagnostics.

tests/conformance/fixtures/iconv_phase1.json
  * Adds `"description"` + `"spec_reference"` top-level fields
    ("POSIX iconv_open, iconv, iconv_close") matching the metadata
    convention already used by the other phase-1 fixtures.

.beads/issues.jsonl
  * Single-line flush reflecting the matrix envelope rollout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-h5x.4","title":"bd-h5x follow-up: internal errno routing for replacement-guard backlog","description":"Scope: remove host glibc errno TLS accesses from replacement-sensitive ABI code by replacing direct libc::__errno_location usage with FrankenLibC's internal errno ABI accessor or setter where semantics are unchanged. Initial slice covers glibc_internal_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, pthread_abi.rs, and rpc_abi.rs. Acceptance: replacement guard forbidden-callthrough count drops materially; targeted cargo tests and replacement guard/census checks pass for the touched logic; bead comment records exact verification commands and representative guard delta.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T22:53:45.146848841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T04:28:57.073095587Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T04:28:57.070247412Z","close_reason":"Deliverables in working tree from Codex swarm. Code review + evidence artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","errno","pthread","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T22:53:45.146848841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1412,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.4","author":"Agent1","text":"Agent1 verification (2026-04-15):

## Errno Routing Migration Complete

Commit 57b0825 converted all raw `*crate::errno_abi::__errno_location() = val` patterns to `crate::errno_abi::set_abi_errno(val)` across:
- glibc_internal_abi.rs (102 call sites)
- err_abi.rs (1 call site)
- unistd_abi.rs (1 call site, ftok null pathname guard)

The only remaining libc::__errno_location usage is inside set_abi_errno() itself (errno_abi.rs:53), which intentionally syncs our errno with host libc in interpose mode.

## Verification Commands
- Check no raw errno writes remain: grep returns 0
- set_abi_errno adoptions: 104 in glibc_internal_abi.rs
- errno_abi module tests: 2 passed
- Replacement guard census: call-throughs dropped from 289 to 235

## Delta
- Call-throughs: 289 → 235 (-54 symbols, 18.7% reduction)
- errno_abi now encapsulates all errno writes behind single function

 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kan","title":"EPIC: Math Kernel Rebuild (production vs research; alien artifact but relevant)","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Goal: remove the ‘ornamental math’ smell while preserving true alien-artifact quality.

Deliverables:
- Relevance audit: every runtime_math module must justify itself with:
  - which runtime decision it drives
  - invariants + evidence ledger
  - measured benefit vs a simpler baseline
- Split membrane into production core vs opt-in research annex.

Acceptance:
- Default build includes only math that is actively used and measurably beneficial.
- Hot path remains within strict/hardened perf budgets.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"AzureBluff","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:07.104692046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:41:13.176900320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:41:13.176879381Z","close_reason":"Math icklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"},{"id":1103,"issue_id":"bd-kan","author":"AzureBluff","text":"Closure proposal evidence (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): bd-kan objective is now satisfied by completed governance/value-proof/partition chain.

Closed blocker chain now in place:
- bd-24x: relevance ledger (decision linkage mapping)
- bd-2yx: production vs research governance classification
- bd-3tp: value-proof A/B evidence
- bd-545: retirement gate policy
- bd-rqn: production manifest partition + compile-time feature routing baseline
- bd-3ot + bd-3ot.1/.2/.3: admission policy + controller manifest + CI enforcement

Current runtime-math production state:
- production manifest is minimal and explicit:
  - production_modules=25
  - research_only_modules=44
- admission and retirement gates pass with deterministic artifacts/logs.
- controller manifest artifact exists and is machine-checkable.

Validation recap:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh PASS
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh PASS

- scripts/check_math_governance.sh PASS
- rch harness tests for admission/classification/policy/retirement all PASS.

Given the above, this epic’s stated deliverable (“remove ornamental math smell while preserving explainable production core and research annex”) is met.

 {"id":"bd-kcbm","title":"C.2: Wire gshadow parser to getsgent/getsgnam/fgetsgent/sgetsgent ABI exports","description":"## Goal
Replace the 8 gshadow stub functions in pwd_abi.rs (lines 963-1040) with real implementations that read /etc/gshadow using the core parser from bd-ta3b (C.1).

## Functions to Implement (8 total)

### Non-reentrant (4 functions, use static thread-local buffer)
1. getsgent() -> *mut sgrp: Sequential enumeration of /etc/gshadow entries. Uses thread-local state tracking current line offset. Returns pointer to static sgrp struct.
2. getsgnam(name) -> *mut sgrp: Lookup by group name. Read /etc/gshadow, find matching entry.
3. fgetsgent(stream) -> *mut sgrp: Read one line from a FILE* stream, parse as gshadow.
4. sgetsgent(string) -> *mut sgrp: Parse a single gshadow-format C string.

### Reentrant _r variants (4 functions, write into caller buffer)
5. getsgent_r(result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int
6. getsgnam_r(name, result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int
7. fgetsgent_r(stream, result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int
8. sgetsgent_r(string, result_buf, buffer, buflen, result) -> c_int

Return 0 on success, ENOENT if not found, ERANGE if buffer too small.

### Also update existing stubs
- setsgent(): Reset enumeration state to beginning of file
- endsgent(): Clear enumeration state and close any cached fd

## struct sgrp Layout (must match glibc <gshadow.h>)
```c
struct sgrp {
    char  *sg_namp;    // group name
    char  *sg_passwd;  // encrypted password
    char **sg_adm;     // NULL-terminated array of administrator names
    char **sg_mem;     // NULL-terminated array of member names
};
```
Size: 32 bytes on x86_64 (4 pointers).

## Implementation Details

### File reading
Read /etc/gshadow via SYS_openat + SYS_read into a stack buffer. Cache content for getsgent enumeration. Same pattern as existing getpwent/getgrent implementations.

### Static buffer for non-_r variants
Thread-local static buffer: sgrp struct + string data + pointer arrays.
```rust
thread_local! {
    static GSHADOW_BUF: RefCell<Vec<u8>> = RefCell::new(vec![0u8; 4096]);
    static GSHADOW_SGRP: Cell<sgrp> = Cell::new(zeroed_sgrp());
}
```

### Buffer packing for _r variants
Pack into caller buffer: name string + NUL, password string + NUL, admin pointers array + NUL ptr, member pointers array + NUL ptr, admin strings + NULs, member strings + NULs. Return ERANGE if total exceeds buflen.

### Handling missing /etc/gshadow
If /etc/gshadow doesn't exist (common on many systems), all functions return null/ENOENT gracefully. This is correct behavior — glibc does the same.

### Comma-separated list splitting
The sg_adm and sg_mem fields in struct sgrp are char** (NULL-terminated pointer arrays). The raw gshadow line has comma-separated names. We must split on commas and create an array of C string pointers within the buffer.

## Unit Tests (in tests/pwd_abi_test.rs)
- test getsgnam_root: getsgnam("root") returns non-null on systems with /etc/gshadow, or null gracefully if file missing
- test getsgnam_nonexistent: getsgnam("zzz_no_such_group_zzz") returns null
- test getsgnam_null_name: getsgnam(NULL) returns null (no crash)
- test getsgent_enumerates: call getsgent repeatedly until null, verify count > 0 (on systems with /etc/gshadow)
- test setsgent_resets_enumeration: setsgent, getsgent, setsgent, getsgent returns same first entry
- test sgetsgent_parses_line: sgetsgent("test:x:admin1:mem1,mem2") returns correct struct
- test sgetsgent_null_returns_null: sgetsgent(NULL) returns null
- test getsgnam_r_erange: getsgnam_r with buflen=1 returns ERANGE
- test getsgnam_r_success: getsgnam_r with large buffer returns 0 and valid struct
- test fgetsgent_null_stream: fgetsgent(NULL) returns null

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_gshadow_native.sh
1. Check if /etc/gshadow exists (skip gracefully if not)
2. Build and run: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pwd_abi_test gshadow
3. If /etc/gshadow exists: also run under LD_PRELOAD to verify getsgnam works
4. Emit structured JSONL report

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=pwd, symbol=getsgnam|getsgent|..., name (if applicable), result_status, errno, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 functions parse /etc/gshadow correctly when it exists
- Graceful null/ENOENT return when /etc/gshadow missing
- _r variants handle ERANGE correctly
- 10+ unit tests pass
- e2e script passes

 {"id":"bd-ta3b","title":"C.1: Implement gshadow line parser in frankenlibc-core","description":"## Goal
Add a gshadow line parser to frankenlibc-core, following the exact pattern of the existing /etc/passwd parser in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

## Reference: Existing Pattern
The passwd parser at pwd/mod.rs uses:
- `pub struct Passwd { pw_name: Vec<u8>, pw_passwd: Vec<u8>, ... }`
- `pub fn parse_passwd_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — splits on colons, validates field count
- `pub fn lookup_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — iterates lines, matches name

The gshadow parser should follow this EXACT pattern for consistency.

## gshadow File Format (/etc/gshadow)
Each line: `group_name:encrypted_password:administrators:members`
- group_name: non-empty, no colons (same as /etc/group name field)
- encrypted_password: may be empty (""), or "!" (locked), or "*" (no password)
- administrators: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no admins)
- members: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no members)

Example lines:
  root:*::
  shadow:!::
  sudo:!::alice,bob
  dev:x:alice:alice,bob,charlie

## Implementation

### File: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs (NEW FILE)
Add `pub mod gshadow;` to crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

### Struct
```rust
pub struct Gshadow {
    pub sg_namp: Vec<u8>,    // group name
    pub sg_passwd: Vec<u8>,  // encrypted password
    pub sg_adm: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated admin list
    pub sg_mem: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated member list
}
```

### Functions
```rust
pub fn parse_gshadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
pub fn lookup_gshadow_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
```

### Parsing Logic
1. Skip empty lines and lines starting with #
2. Split on b':' — must have exactly 4 fields
3. Field 0 (name) must be non-empty
4. Fields 1-3 may be empty
5. Return Gshadow with owned Vec<u8> copies

## Inline Tests (#[cfg(test)])
- parse_valid_full_line: `sudo:!:admin1,admin2:user1,user2` -> all 4 fields correct
- parse_minimal_line: `root:*::` -> password="*", admins="", members=""
- parse_empty_password: `grp:::` -> password is empty
- parse_locked_password: `grp:!::` -> password is "!"
- parse_with_admins_and_members: `dev:x:alice:alice,bob` -> admins="alice", members="alice,bob"
- reject_too_few_fields: `root:*:` (3 colons needed, only 2) -> None
- reject_empty_name: `:*::` -> None
- reject_comment_line: `# comment` -> None
- reject_empty_line: "" -> None
- lookup_finds_entry: content with 3 lines, lookup "sudo" finds correct one
- lookup_returns_none_for_missing: lookup "nonexistent" returns None
- lookup_is_case_sensitive: "ROOT" does not match "root"

## Acceptance Criteria
Parser handles all gshadow field combinations. 12 inline tests pass. cargo clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:37.066192744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.720091402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.719809839Z","close_reason":"Implemented gshadow parser in frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs. Struct Gshadow with sg_namp/sg_passwd/sg_adm/sg_mem fields. parse_gshadow_line() handles comments, blank lines, field validation. lookup_gshadow_by_name() scans content for case-sensitive name match. 14 inline tests pass covering: valid full/minimal lines, empty/locked password, admin+member lists, reject malformed, lookup hit/miss/case sensitivity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pwd","stub-elimination"]}

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}

- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:46.293544458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767467115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767444111Z","close_reason":"Aligned README/FEATURE_PARITY to support taxonomy snapshot; removed full-replacement overclaims; added hybrid interposition truth table","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs"]}
